codeReview.summary: The fix adequately closes the prior scrutiny gap. Commit 790fb0f extends the same two attacker-isolation HTTP tests to prove the victim/owner can still read their own entry-by-id facts after the attacker-facing denial checks, which is the exact missing transport-boundary proof called out in the failed review for commit 8334739.

summary: Pass: reviewed the prior failing diff and the fix diff, plus the worker transcript skeleton and handoff. The new assertions in apps/backend/internal/bootstrap/public_track_cross_user_security_test.go add owner-side entry-by-id readback after attacker denial for both current-timer and history scenarios, so the same seeded HTTP boundary now proves both attacker exclusion and owner-only visibility without reopening unrelated scope.
